page_content:
PHYS708 Seminar in Teaching College Physics (1 Credit)

PHYS709 Seminar in General Physics (1 Credit)

PHYS625 Quantum Many-Body Theory I (3 Credits) Non-relativistic second quantization, path integrals and functional ﬁeld integrals, perturbation theory with Feynman diagrams, interacting electron gas. Prerequisite: PHYS612 and PHYS613.

PHYS715 Chaotic Dynamics (3 Credits) Theory and applications of chaos in dynamical systems including such topics as strange attractors, Lyapanov exponents, quasiperiodicity, period doubling, intermittency, crises, fractal basin boundaries, chaotic scattering, KAM tori, and quantum chaos. Prerequisite: PHYS601.

PHYS626 Quantum Many-Body Theory II (3 Credits) Continuation of PHYS625. Functional-integral formulation of quantum ﬁeld theory. Phase transitions and broken symmetry. Magnetism, superconductivity, and other applications. The renormalization group. Prerequisite: PHYS612, PHYS613, and PHYS625.
